content.full_text:
DEAN MUNRO
10180 Firwood Drive
Cupertino, California 95014
(408) 277-5823

April 9, 1990

VIA TELECOPY

Blanca M. Breeze, Esq.
Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Advice

Dear Ms. Breeze:

This letter requests confirmation of our telephone
conversation of April 4, 1990.

In our conversation I explained that I previously obtained an
advice letter from the Fair Political Practices Commission
regarding my work as a Project Director for the Sports Advisory
Committee for the City of San Jose ("SAC"). A copy of such advice
letter is enclosed herewith. By way of that letter, it was informed
that I did not have a prohibited conflict of interest as a result
of my taking a leave of absence from my position as Chief of Staff
for the Mayor of San Jose ("Mayor") to undertake duties of Project
Director to promote San Jose to the National Hockey League. The
underlying principle of such advice was that the Mayor was not a
source of income to me, and I had no conflict if my actions would
not benefit a source of income. For your convenience my previous
letters to the FPPC, dated September 21, 1989, and October 4, 1989,
which request advice and outline the facts of that situation are
enclosed herewith.

In our conversation, I further explained that as of May 1,
1990, I will again undertake the position of the Mayor's Chief of
Staff. However, in such position I still will not receive any
payments or compensation from Mayor McEnery personally. What I
would like to do, however, is retain my association with SAC and
render services to SAC a few hours per week. In our telephone
conversation you indicated that based on such facts I would not
have a prohibited conflict of interest. Please confirm that
conclusion in writing as soon as possible.

I would also appreciate the FPPC's analysis and advice as to
whether I may participate in the San Jose Sports Authority (the
"Authority"). The Authority is a new entity (separate from SAC)

formed and founded by the City of San Jose to market San Jose as
a general sports venue. The Mayor will be involved only to the
extent that he will nominate Authority members. It is my belief
that such participation would not be prohibited because my
activities would be directly analogous to those taken as a Project
Director for SAC. In one position I will promote San Jose to the
NHL, and in the other I will promote San Jose to a variety of other
sporting interests. The effects on the Mayor will be similar in
nature, but the Mayor would still not become a source of income to
me. Therefore, I believe the analysis is the same. Please provide
advice on this issue also.

Thank you very much for your help in this matter.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

DEAN MUNRO

DM:BDB:sc
Enclosures
\BB\L\McEne0101.BRE

DEC 05 '89 16:00 BERLINER+SAN JOSE

P.1

DEAN MUNRO
10180 Firwood Drive
Cupertino, California 95014
(408) 277-5823


October 5, 1989

Katherine Donovan, Esq.
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Advice

Dear Ms. Donovan:

This letter is delivered to clarify a certain fact
set forth in my request for advice dated September 21, 1989.
Although I do not believe it will affect the analysis in this
situation, please note that the contractual amendment under which
I have assumed the role of Project Director terminates as of
December 31, 1989. My leave of absence from my position as Mayor
on that date. It appears, however, that my contract will be
extended into 1990 in light of the fact that the NHL is not
expected to make a decision as to which cities will be granted
expansion franchises until mid-1990.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Very truly yours,

JM:BDB:sg                DEAN MUNRO

DEAN MUNRO
10180 Firwood Drive
Cupertino, California 95014
(408) 277-5823


September 21, 1989


Katherine Donovan, Esq.
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

     Re:  Request for Advice

Dear Ms. Donovan:

     This letter is to request advice regarding my ability to
participate in certain governmental decisions in light of the
Conflict of Interest provisions of the Political Reform Act of
1974.

Question:  By virtue of my previous association as Chief of
Staff for the Mayor of San Jose, am I prohibited from fulfilling
my current duties as a Project Director for the Sports Advisory
Committee of the City of San Jose as described below?

FACTS

Association with the Mayor of San Jose

     For approximately seven years, I was the Chief of Mayor Tom
McEnery's staff and worked closely with Mayor McEnery on a day-
to-day basis.  As the Mayor's Chief of Staff, I reported to
Mayor McEnery and was compensated by the City of San Jose
through the Mayor's budget.

     Pursuant to a Stipulated Permanent Injunction, Mayor McEnery
is prohibited from, with certain exceptions, participating in or
influencing  decisions  affecting  the  planned  downtown  arena
project in the City of San Jose.  The injunction arises out of
potential conflicts of interest resulting from the Mayor's
interest in certain business enterprises and real property
located in downtown San Jose, within a mile of the planned arena.
I am not a party to or directly bound by such injunction.

Katherine Donovan, Esq.
September 21, 1989
Page 2

Project Director Position

The City Council of San Jose has formed the Sports Authority
to promote San Jose as a venue for amateur and professional
athletics. I have agreed to take a position as Project Director
to coordinate the City's activities in promoting San Jose to the
National Hockey League. In that capacity, I will report to San
Jose's City Manager. While I am Project Director I will be on
an indefinite leave of absence from my duties as the Mayor's
Chief of Staff. During this leave of absence, I will not be
receiving any compensation from the Mayor's budget, nor will I
have any reporting responsibilities to the Mayor.

No Independent Conflict

I understand that pursuant to the stipulated injunction
mentioned above, Mayor McEnery will not be able to make certain
governmental decisions with respect to the planned downtown
arena, which decisions may become part of my responsibilities as
Project Director. However, I have no independent conflict of
interest; I have no financial interest or source of income which
would be affected by the planned downtown arena or a National
Hockey League decision to place a franchise in such arena.
Neither Mayor McEnery nor any entity with which he is associated
is a source of income to me. In addition, I will not receive any
direction from Mayor McEnery with respect to my activities as
Project Director.

It is my understanding that Mayor McEnery's conflict of
interest and the restrictions on his activities imposed by the
above-mentioned injunction cannot be imputed to me. Thus,
although the Mayor may not attempt to influence my decisions as
Project Director, it is my belief that I am not prohibited by the
Political Reform Act of 1974 from fulfilling my complete duties
as Project Director. However, I would like you to confirm that
no conflict arises merely by virtue of my prior close association
with Mayor McEnery. As I am already under contract as Project
Director and have assumed those job duties, your prompt response
is appreciated. If a verbal response is available first, please
respond by phone prior to your delivery of a written response.

Thank you very much for your help in this matter.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

DEAN MUNRO
